title: Xtra Crumbly Algerian Lamb Tagine
description: A traditional Algerian dish featuring tender lamb and a blend of aromatic spices, with a crumbly texture from fermented ingredients, perfect for a lazy Sunday afternoon. This recipe is gluten-free friendly and incorporates white vinegar for added flavor.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, white vinegar, cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper to make the marinade.
Add the lamb pieces to the marinade and mix well to coat, then cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Remove the lamb from the marinade, letting any excess liquid drip off, and brown the lamb pieces on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until it is softened and lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add 1 cup of water to the pot and bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Use two forks to shred the lamb into bite-sized pieces and return it to the pot.
Simmer the tagine, uncovered, for an additional 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has thickened and the lamb is very tender.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
